v.CNV: 不要严厉责备老年人，却要劝他好像劝父亲；劝青年人好像劝弟兄；
v.ESV: Do not rebuke an older man but encourage him as you would a father, younger men as brothers,
v.GAE: 늙은이를 꾸짖지 말고 권하되 아버지에게 하듯 하며 젊은이에게는 형제에게 하듯 하고
v.KJV: Rebuke not an elder, but intreat him as a father; and the younger men as brethren;
v.NASB: Do not sharply rebuke an older man, but rather appeal to him as a father, to the younger men as brothers,
v.NIV: Do not rebuke an older man harshly, but exhort him as if he were your father. Treat younger men as brothers,
v.NRSV: Do not speak harshly to an older man, but speak to him as to a father, to younger men as brothers,
v.SAENEW: 나이가 많은 이를 나무라지 말고, 아버지를 대하듯이 권면하십시오. 젊은 남자는 형제를 대하듯이 권면하십시오.
v.WLB: 그대는 나이가 많은 남자를 꾸짖지 말고 아버지에게 하듯 권면하여라. 청년들에게는 형제들을 대하듯이 권면하여라.

v.CNV: 劝年老的妇人好像劝母亲；存着纯洁的心，劝青年女子好像劝姊妹。
v.ESV: older women as mothers, younger women as sisters, in all purity.
v.GAE: 늙은 여자에게는 어머니에게 하듯 하며 젊은 여자에게는 온전히 깨끗함으로 자매에게 하듯 하라
v.KJV: The elder women as mothers; the younger as sisters, with all purity.
v.NASB: the older women as mothers, and the younger women as sisters, in all purity.
v.NIV: older women as mothers, and younger women as sisters, with absolute purity.
v.NRSV: to older women as mothers, to younger women as sisters — with absolute purity.
v.SAENEW: 나이가 많은 여자는 어머니를 대하듯이 권면하고, 젊은 여자는 자매를 대하듯이, 오로지 순결한 마음으로 권면하십시오.
v.WLB: 나이 많은 여자들에게는 어머니를 대하듯이 권면하고 젊은 여자들에게는 자매를 대하듯이 오직 순수함으로 권면하여라.

v.CNV: 要敬重供养那些无依无靠的寡妇。
v.ESV: Honor widows who are truly widows.
v.GAE: 참 과부인 과부를 존대하라
v.KJV: Honour widows that are widows indeed.
v.NASB: Honor widows who are widows indeed;
v.NIV: Give proper recognition to those widows who are really in need.
v.NRSV: Honor widows who are really widows.
v.SAENEW: 참 과부인 과부를 존대하십시오.
v.WLB: 참 과부인 과부를 존대하여라.

v.CNV: 寡妇若有儿孙，就应当让儿孙先在自己家里学习孝道，报答亲恩，因为这在　神面前是蒙悦纳的。
v.ESV: But if a widow has children or grandchildren, let them first learn to show godliness to their own household and to make some return to their parents, for this is pleasing in the sight of God.
v.GAE: 만일 어떤 과부에게 자녀나 손자들이 있거든 그들로 먼저 자기 집에서 효를 행하여 부모에게 보답하기를 배우게 하라 이것이 하나님 앞에 받으실 만한 것이니라
v.KJV: But if any widow have children or nephews, let them learn first to shew piety at home, and to requite their parents: for that is good and acceptable before God.
v.NASB: but if any widow has children or grandchildren, they must first learn to practice piety in regard to their own family and to make some return to their parents; for this is acceptable in the sight of God.
v.NIV: But if a widow has children or grandchildren, these should learn first of all to put their religion into practice by caring for their own family and so repaying their parents and grandparents, for this is pleasing to God.
v.NRSV: If a widow has children or grandchildren, they should first learn their religious duty to their own family and make some repayment to their parents; for this is pleasing in God’s sight.
v.SAENEW: 어떤 과부에게 자녀들이나 손자들이 있으면, 그들은 먼저 자기네 가족에게 종교상의 의무를 행하는 것을 배워야 하고, 어버이에게 보답하는 것을 배워야 합니다. 이것이 바로 하나님께서 그들에게 원하시는 일입니다.
v.WLB: 그러나 어떤 과부에게 자녀나 손자가 있다면 그들로 하여금 먼저 자기 집에서 신앙적 의무를 다하는 것과 부모에게 보답하는 것을 배우게 하여라. 이것이 하나님께서 원하시는 일이다.

v.CNV: 那无依无靠独居的寡妇，她们仰望　神，昼夜不住地祈求祷告。
v.ESV: She who is truly a widow, left all alone, has set her hope on God and continues in supplications and prayers night and day,
v.GAE: 참 과부로서 외로운 자는 하나님께 소망을 두어 주야로 항상 간구와 기도를 하거니와
v.KJV: Now she that is a widow indeed, and desolate, trusteth in God, and continueth in supplications and prayers night and day.
v.NASB: Now she who is a widow indeed and who has been left alone, has fixed her hope on God and continues in entreaties and prayers night and day.
v.NIV: The widow who is really in need and left all alone puts her hope in God and continues night and day to pray and to ask God for help.
v.NRSV: The real widow, left alone, has set her hope on God and continues in supplications and prayers night and day;
v.SAENEW: 참 과부로서 의지할 데가 없는 이는, 하나님께 소망을 두고, 밤낮으로 끊임없이 간구와 기도를 드립니다.
v.WLB: 참 과부로서 외로운 사람은 자기의 소망을 하나님께 두고 밤낮으로 기도와 간구에 전념한다.

v.CNV: 寡妇若奢侈宴乐，虽然活着也是死的。
v.ESV: but she who is self-indulgent is dead even while she lives.
v.GAE: 향락을 좋아하는 자는 살았으나 죽었느니라
v.KJV: But she that liveth in pleasure is dead while she liveth.
v.NASB: But she who gives herself to wanton pleasure is dead even while she lives.
v.NIV: But the widow who lives for pleasure is dead even while she lives.
v.NRSV: but the widow who lives for pleasure is dead even while she lives.
v.SAENEW: 향락에 빠져서 사는 과부는, 살아 있으나 죽은 것입니다.
v.WLB: 향락을 좋아하는 여자는 살아 있으나 죽은 것이다.

v.CNV: 这些事你要嘱咐她们，使她们无可指摘。
v.ESV: Command these things as well, so that they may be without reproach.
v.GAE: 네가 또한 이것을 명하여 그들로 책망 받을 것이 없게 하라
v.KJV: And these things give in charge, that they may be blameless.
v.NASB: Prescribe these things as well, so that they may be above reproach.
v.NIV: Give the people these instructions, so that no one may be open to blame.
v.NRSV: Give these commands as well, so that they may be above reproach.
v.SAENEW: 그들에게 이런 것을 명령하여, 그들이 비난을 받는 일이 없도록 하십시오.
v.WLB: 또한 그대는 이것들을 명령해서 그들로 비난받는 일이 없도록 하여라.

v.CNV: 人若连自己的家人也不供养，就是背弃信仰，比不信的人还不如。
v.ESV: But if anyone does not provide for his relatives, and especially for members of his household, he has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
v.GAE: 누구든지 자기 친족 특히 자기 가족을 돌보지 아니하면 믿음을 배반한 자요 불신자보다 더 악한 자니라
v.KJV: But if any provide not for his own, and specially for those of his own house, he hath denied the faith, and is worse than an infidel.
v.NASB: But if anyone does not provide for his own, and especially for those of his household, he has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
v.NIV: Anyone who does not provide for their relatives, and especially for their own household, has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
v.NRSV: And whoever does not provide for relatives, and especially for family members, has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
v.SAENEW: 누구든지 자기 친척 특히 가족을 돌보지 않으면, 그는 벌써 믿음을 저버린 사람이요, 믿지 않는 사람보다 더 나쁜 사람입니다.
v.WLB: 누구든지 자기 친척 특히 자기 가족을 돌보지 않는 사람은 믿음을 저버린 사람이요. 믿지 않는 사람보다 더 악한 사람이다.

v.CNV: 寡妇登记，年纪必须到达六十岁，只作过一个丈夫的妻子，
v.ESV: Let a widow be enrolled if she is not less than sixty years of age, having been the wife of one husband,
v.GAE: 과부로 명부에 올릴 자는 나이가 육십이 덜 되지 아니하고 한 남편의 아내였던 자로서
v.KJV: Let not a widow be taken into the number under threescore years old, having been the wife of one man,
v.NASB: A widow is to be put on the list only if she is not less than sixty years old, having been the wife of one man,
v.NIV: No widow may be put on the list of widows unless she is over sixty, has been faithful to her husband,
v.NRSV: Let a widow be put on the list if she is not less than sixty years old and has been married only once;
v.SAENEW: 과부로 명부에 올릴 이는, 예순 살이 덜 되어서는 안되고, 한 남편의 아내였던 사람이라야 합니다.
v.WLB: 과부로 명부에 올릴 사람은 60세 이상이어야 하고 한 남편의 아내였던 사람이어야 한다.

v.CNV: 并且要有善行的见证：就如养育儿女，接待客旅，替圣徒洗脚，救济困苦的人，尽力行各样的善事等。
v.ESV: and having a reputation for good works: if she has brought up children, has shown hospitality, has washed the feet of the saints, has cared for the afflicted, and has devoted herself to every good work.
v.GAE: 선한 행실의 증거가 있어 혹은 자녀를 양육하며 혹은 나그네를 대접하며 혹은 성도들의 발을 씻으며 혹은 환난 당한 자들을 구제하며 혹은 모든 선한 일을 행한 자라야 할 것이요
v.KJV: Well reported of for good works; if she have brought up children, if she have lodged strangers, if she have washed the saints’ feet, if she have relieved the afflicted, if she have diligently followed every good work.
v.NASB: having a reputation for good works; and if she has brought up children, if she has shown hospitality to strangers, if she has washed the saints' feet, if she has assisted those in distress, and if she has devoted herself to every good work.
v.NIV: and is well known for her good deeds, such as bringing up children, showing hospitality, washing the feet of the Lord’s people, helping those in trouble and devoting herself to all kinds of good deeds.
v.NRSV: she must be well attested for her good works, as one who has brought up children, shown hospitality, washed the saints’ feet, helped the afflicted, and devoted herself to doing good in every way.
v.SAENEW: 그는 착한 행실을 인정받는 사람이라야 하는데, 자녀를 잘 기르거나, 나그네를 잘 대접하거나, 성도들을 자기 집에 모시거나, 어려움을 당한 사람을 도와주거나, 모든 선한 일에 몸을 바친 사람이라야 합니다.
v.WLB: 또 그는 선한 행실들로 인정을 받는 사람이어야 한다. 곧 자녀를 잘 부양했든지 나그네를 잘 대접했든지 성도들의 발을 씻겼든지 환난당한 사람들을 구제했든지 모든 선한 일에 헌신한 사람이어야 한다.

v.CNV: 至于年轻的寡妇，不要给她们登记，因为她们一旦情欲冲动而背弃基督的时候，就想结婚。
v.ESV: But refuse to enroll younger widows, for when their passions draw them away from Christ, they desire to marry
v.GAE: 젊은 과부는 올리지 말지니 이는 정욕으로 그리스도를 배반할 때에 시집 가고자 함이니
v.KJV: But the younger widows refuse: for when they have begun to wax wanton against Christ, they will marry;
v.NASB: But refuse to put younger widows on the list, for when they feel sensual desires in disregard of Christ, they want to get married,
v.NIV: As for younger widows, do not put them on such a list. For when their sensual desires overcome their dedication to Christ, they want to marry.
v.NRSV: But refuse to put younger widows on the list; for when their sensual desires alienate them from Christ, they want to marry,
v.SAENEW: 젊은 과부는 명단에 올리는 것을 거절하십시오. 그들은, 그리스도를 거슬러 정욕에 이끌리면 결혼을 하고 싶어할 것이고,
v.WLB: 그러나 젊은 과부는 거절하여라. 이는 그들이 그리스도를 거슬러 정욕에 사로잡히게 되면 결혼하고 싶어 할 것이고

v.CNV: 她们被定罪，是因为丢弃了起初的信誓。
v.ESV: and so incur condemnation for having abandoned their former faith.
v.GAE: 처음 믿음을 저버렸으므로 정죄를 받느니라
v.KJV: Having damnation, because they have cast off their first faith.
v.NASB: thus incurring condemnation, because they have set aside their previous pledge.
v.NIV: Thus they bring judgment on themselves, because they have broken their first pledge.
v.NRSV: and so they incur condemnation for having violated their first pledge.
v.SAENEW: 처음 서약을 저버렸기 때문에 비난을 받을 것입니다.
v.WLB: 그럴 경우 처음 믿음을 저버렸기 때문에 심판을 받게 될 것이다.

v.CNV: 同时，她们懒惰惯了，挨家闲游；不但懒惰，而且好说闲话，好管闲事，说不该说的话。
v.ESV: Besides that, they learn to be idlers, going about from house to house, and not only idlers, but also gossips and busybodies, saying what they should not.
v.GAE: 또 그들은 게으름을 익혀 집집으로 돌아 다니고 게으를 뿐 아니라 쓸데없는 말을 하며 일을 만들며 마땅히 아니할 말을 하나니
v.KJV: And withal they learn to be idle, wandering about from house to house; and not only idle, but tattlers also and busybodies, speaking things which they ought not.
v.NASB: At the same time they also learn to be idle, as they go around from house to house; and not merely idle, but also gossips and busybodies, talking about things not proper to mention.
v.NIV: Besides, they get into the habit of being idle and going about from house to house. And not only do they become idlers, but also busybodies who talk nonsense, saying things they ought not to.
v.NRSV: Besides that, they learn to be idle, gadding about from house to house; and they are not merely idle, but also gossips and busybodies, saying what they should not say.
v.SAENEW: 또한 그들은 이 집 저 집 돌아다니면서 빈둥거리는 것을 익힐 것입니다. 더욱이, 그들은 빈둥거릴 뿐만 아니라, 수다를 떨고, 남의 일에 참견하고, 해서는 안 되는 말을 할 것입니다.
v.WLB: 또한 그들은 이 집 저 집 돌아다니며 게으름을 배우고 게으를 뿐 아니라 수다를 떨며 남의 일을 참견하고 마땅히 해서는 안 될 말을 할 것이다.

v.CNV: 因此，我愿年轻的寡妇结婚，生儿育女，管理家务，不给敌人有辱骂的机会；
v.ESV: So I would have younger widows marry, bear children, manage their households, and give the adversary no occasion for slander.
v.GAE: 그러므로 젊은이는 시집 가서 아이를 낳고 집을 다스리고 대적에게 비방할 기회를 조금도 주지 말기를 원하노라
v.KJV: I will therefore that the younger women marry, bear children, guide the house, give none occasion to the adversary to speak reproachfully.
v.NASB: Therefore, I want younger widows to get married, bear children, keep house, and give the enemy no occasion for reproach;
v.NIV: So I counsel younger widows to marry, to have children, to manage their homes and to give the enemy no opportunity for slander.
v.NRSV: So I would have younger widows marry, bear children, and manage their households, so as to give the adversary no occasion to revile us.
v.SAENEW: 그러므로 젊은 과부들은 재혼을 해서, 아이를 낳고, 가정을 다스려서, 적대자들에게 비방할 기회를 조금도 주지 말기를 바랍니다.
v.WLB: 그러므로 나는 젊은 과부들은 결혼해서 아이를 낳고 집안일을 돌봄으로 대적자에게 비난할 기회를 아예 주지 않기를 바란다.

v.CNV: 因为有些人已经转去跟从撒但了。
v.ESV: For some have already strayed after Satan.
v.GAE: 이미 사탄에게 돌아간 자들도 있도다
v.KJV: For some are already turned aside after Satan.
v.NASB: for some have already turned aside to follow Satan.
v.NIV: Some have in fact already turned away to follow Satan.
v.NRSV: For some have already turned away to follow Satan.
v.SAENEW: 어떤 과부들은 이미 곁길로 나가서, 사탄을 따라갔습니다.
v.WLB: 실제로 어떤 사람들은 이미 떠나 사탄에게로 갔다.

v.CNV: 如果信主的妇女家中有寡妇，她就应该救济她们，不要让教会受累。这样，教会就可以救济那些无依无靠的寡妇了。
v.ESV: If any believing woman has relatives who are widows, let her care for them. Let the church not be burdened, so that it may care for those who are truly widows.
v.GAE: 만일 믿는 여자에게 과부 친척이 있거든 자기가 도와 주고 교회가 짐지지 않게 하라 이는 참 과부를 도와 주게 하려 함이라
v.KJV: If any man or woman that believeth have widows, let them relieve them, and let not the church be charged; that it may relieve them that are widows indeed.
v.NASB: If any woman who is a believer has dependent widows, she must assist them and the church must not be burdened, so that it may assist those who are widows indeed.
v.NIV: If any woman who is a believer has widows in her care, she should continue to help them and not let the church be burdened with them, so that the church can help those widows who are really in need.
v.NRSV: If any believing woman has relatives who are really widows, let her assist them; let the church not be burdened, so that it can assist those who are real widows.
v.SAENEW: 어떤 여신도의 집안에 과부들이 있거든, 그 여신도가 그들을 도와주어야 할 것이요, 교회에 짐을 지우지 말아야 할 것입니다. 그렇게 하여야 교회가 참 과부들을 도울 수 있을 것입니다.
v.WLB: 만일 믿는 여자에게 과부 친척이 있다면 자기가 그 과부를 도와주고 교회에 부담을 주지 않도록 하여라. 이는 교회가 참 과부를 도와주도록 하기 위한 것이다.

v.CNV: 那些善于治理教会的长老，尤其是那些在讲道和教导上劳苦的长老，你们应当看他们是配受加倍的敬重和供奉的。
v.ESV: Let the elders who rule well be considered worthy of double honor, especially those who labor in preaching and teaching.
v.GAE: 잘 다스리는 장로들은 배나 존경할 자로 알되 말씀과 가르침에 수고하는 이들에게는 더욱 그리할 것이니라
v.KJV: Let the elders that rule well be counted worthy of double honour, especially they who labour in the word and doctrine.
v.NASB: The elders who rule well are to be considered worthy of double honor, especially those who work hard at preaching and teaching.
v.NIV: The elders who direct the affairs of the church well are worthy of double honor, especially those whose work is preaching and teaching.
v.NRSV: Let the elders who rule well be considered worthy of double honor, especially those who labor in preaching and teaching;
v.SAENEW: 잘 다스리는 장로들은 두 배로 존경을 받아야 합니다. 특히 말씀을 전파하는 일과 가르치는 일에 수고하는 장로들은 더욱 그러하여야 합니다.
v.WLB: 잘 다스리는 장로들은 두 배나 존경을 받게 하여라. 특히 설교와 가르치는 일에 수고하는 사람들을 더욱 그렇게 하여라.

v.CNV: 因为经上说：“牛踹谷的时候，不可笼住它的嘴。”又说：“作工的配得工价。”
v.ESV: For the Scripture says, “You shall not muzzle an ox when it treads out the grain,” and, “The laborer deserves his wages.”
v.GAE: 성경에 일렀으되 곡식을 밟아 떠는 소의 입에 망을 씌우지 말라 하였고 또 일꾼이 그 삯을 받는 것은 마땅하다 하였느니라
v.KJV: For the scripture saith, Thou shalt not muzzle the ox that treadeth out the corn. And, The labourer is worthy of his reward.
v.NASB: For the Scripture says, 'YOU SHALL NOT MUZZLE THE OX WHILE HE IS THRESHING,' and 'The laborer is worthy of his wages.'
v.NIV: For Scripture says, “Do not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain,” and “The worker deserves his wages.”
v.NRSV: for the scripture says, “You shall not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain,” and, “The laborer deserves to be paid.”
v.SAENEW: 성경에 이르기를, "타작 마당에서 낟알을 밟아 떠는 소의 입에 망을 씌우지 말라" 하였고, "일꾼이 자기 삯을 받는 것은 마땅하다" 하였습니다.
v.WLB: 성경에서 말하기를 “곡식을 밟아 떠는 소의 입에 망을 씌우지 말라”고 했고, “일꾼이 자기의 품삯을 받는 것은 마땅하다”고 했다.

v.CNV: 控诉长老的事，除非有两三个证人，否则不要受理。
v.ESV: Do not admit a charge against an elder except on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
v.GAE: 장로에 대한 고발은 두세 증인이 없으면 받지 말 것이요
v.KJV: Against an elder receive not an accusation, but before two or three witnesses.
v.NASB: Do not receive an accusation against an elder except on the basis of two or three witnesses.
v.NIV: Do not entertain an accusation against an elder unless it is brought by two or three witnesses.
v.NRSV: Never accept any accusation against an elder except on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
v.SAENEW: 장로에 대한 고발은 두 사람이나 세 사람의 증인이 없이는 받아들이지 마십시오.
v.WLB: 장로에 대한 고소는 두세 사람의 증인이 없으면 받지 마라.

v.CNV: 常常犯罪的，你要当众责备他们，使其余的人也有所惧怕。
v.ESV: As for those who persist in sin, rebuke them in the presence of all, so that the rest may stand in fear.
v.GAE: 범죄한 자들을 모든 사람 앞에서 꾸짖어 나머지 사람들로 두려워하게 하라
v.KJV: Them that sin rebuke before all, that others also may fear.
v.NASB: Those who continue in sin, rebuke in the presence of all, so that the rest also will be fearful of sinning.
v.NIV: But those elders who are sinning you are to reprove before everyone, so that the others may take warning.
v.NRSV: As for those who persist in sin, rebuke them in the presence of all, so that the rest also may stand in fear.
v.SAENEW: 죄를 짓는 사람을 모든 사람 앞에서 꾸짖어서, 나머지 사람들도 두려워하게 하십시오.
v.WLB: 죄를 지은 사람은 모든 사람 앞에서 꾸짖어 다른 사람들도 두려워하게 하여라.

v.CNV: 我在　神和基督耶稣以及蒙拣选的天使面前叮嘱你，要毫无成见地持守这些话，行事也不要偏心。
v.ESV: In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus and of the elect angels I charge you to keep these rules without prejudging, doing nothing from partiality.
v.GAE: 하나님과 그리스도 예수와 택하심을 받은 천사들 앞에서 내가 엄히 명하노니 너는 편견이 없이 이것들을 지켜 아무 일도 불공평하게 하지 말며
v.KJV: I charge thee before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, and the elect angels, that thou observe these things without preferring one before another, doing nothing by partiality.
v.NASB: I solemnly charge you in the presence of God and of Christ Jesus and of His chosen angels, to maintain these principles without bias, doing nothing in a spirit of partiality.
v.NIV: I charge you, in the sight of God and Christ Jesus and the elect angels, to keep these instructions without partiality, and to do nothing out of favoritism.
v.NRSV: In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus and of the elect angels, I warn you to keep these instructions without prejudice, doing nothing on the basis of partiality.
v.SAENEW: 하나님과 그리스도 예수와 택하심을 받은 천사들 앞에서 내가 엄숙히 명령합니다. 그대는 편견 없이 이것들을 지키고, 어떤 일이든지 공평하게 처리하십시오.
v.WLB: 내가 하나님과 그리스도 예수와 택하심을 받은 천사들 앞에서 엄숙히 명령한다. 그대는 이것들을 편견 없이 지키고 아무 일도 불공평하게 처리하지 마라.

v.CNV: 不要匆匆忙忙地替别人按手，也不要在别人的罪上有分，要保守自己清洁。
v.ESV: Do not be hasty in the laying on of hands, nor take part in the sins of others; keep yourself pure.
v.GAE: 아무에게나 경솔히 안수하지 말고 다른 사람의 죄에 간섭하지 말며 네 자신을 지켜 정결하게 하라
v.KJV: Lay hands suddenly on no man, neither be partaker of other men’s sins: keep thyself pure.
v.NASB: Do not lay hands upon anyone too hastily and thereby share responsibility for the sins of others; keep yourself free from sin.
v.NIV: Do not be hasty in the laying on of hands, and do not share in the sins of others. Keep yourself pure.
v.NRSV: Do not ordain anyone hastily, and do not participate in the sins of others; keep yourself pure.
v.SAENEW: 아무에게나 경솔하게 안수하지 마십시오. 남의 죄에 끼여들지 말고, 자기를 깨끗하게 지키십시오.
v.WLB: 아무에게나 경솔하게 안수하지 마라. 다른 사람들의 죄에 동참하지 말고 그대 자신을 깨끗하게 지켜라.

v.CNV: 因为你的胃不好，而且身体常常软弱，不要单单喝水，可以稍微用点酒。
v.ESV: No longer drink only water, but use a little wine for the sake of your stomach and your frequent ailments.
v.GAE: 이제부터는 물만 마시지 말고 네 위장과 자주 나는 병을 위하여는 포도주를 조금씩 쓰라
v.KJV: Drink no longer water, but use a little wine for thy stomach’s sake and thine often infirmities.
v.NASB: No longer drink water exclusively, but use a little wine for the sake of your stomach and your frequent ailments.
v.NIV: Stop drinking only water, and use a little wine because of your stomach and your frequent illnesses.
v.NRSV: No longer drink only water, but take a little wine for the sake of your stomach and your frequent ailments.
v.SAENEW: 이제부터는 물만 마시지 말고, 위장과 잦은 병을 생각해서 포도주를 조금씩 쓰십시오.
v.WLB: 이제부터는 물만 마시지 말고 그대의 위장과 잦은 병을 생각해 포도주도 조금씩 먹도록 하여라.

v.CNV: 有些人的罪是明显的，就先受审判；有些人的罪是后来才显露出来的。
v.ESV: The sins of some people are conspicuous, going before them to judgment, but the sins of others appear later.
v.GAE: 어떤 사람들의 죄는 밝히 드러나 먼저 심판에 나아가고 어떤 사람들의 죄는 그 뒤를 따르나니
v.KJV: Some men’s sins are open beforehand, going before to judgment; and some men they follow after.
v.NASB: The sins of some men are quite evident, going before them to judgment; for others, their sins follow after.
v.NIV: The sins of some are obvious, reaching the place of judgment ahead of them; the sins of others trail behind them.
v.NRSV: The sins of some people are conspicuous and precede them to judgment, while the sins of others follow them there.
v.SAENEW: 어떤 사람들의 죄는 명백해서, 재판을 받기 전에 먼저 드러나고, 어떤 사람들의 죄는 나중에야 드러납니다.
v.WLB: 어떤 사람들의 죄는 분명해서 먼저 심판을 받고 또한 어떤 사람들의 죄는 그 다음에 심판을 받는다.

v.CNV: 照样，善行也是明显的，就算不明显，也不能隐藏。
v.ESV: So also good works are conspicuous, and even those that are not cannot remain hidden.
v.GAE: 이와 같이 선행도 밝히 드러나고 그렇지 아니한 것도 숨길 수 없느니라
v.KJV: Likewise also the good works of some are manifest beforehand; and they that are otherwise cannot be hid.
v.NASB: Likewise also, deeds that are good are quite evident, and those which are otherwise cannot be concealed.
v.NIV: In the same way, good deeds are obvious, and even those that are not obvious cannot remain hidden forever.
v.NRSV: So also good works are conspicuous; and even when they are not, they cannot remain hidden.
v.SAENEW: 이와 마찬가지로, 착한 행실도 드러나게 마련이고, 드러나지 않은 것도, 언제까지나 감추어져 있지는 못합니다.
v.WLB: 이와 같이 선한 일들도 명백히 드러나고 그렇지 않은 것들도 숨길 수 없다.
